title: Fanout Pub/Sub and Subscriber Responsibilities · Execution Trace: Late Subscriber Semantics
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must prove one queue per responsibility, late-subscriber behavior, cancellation, and independent consumer ownership; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the late subscriber semantics increment in a Go subscriber responsibility and queue lifecycle map. Learners pr…
objectives: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Design durable subscription queues with retention, offline backlog, capacity, replay alternatives, and ownership limits; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Late Subscriber Semantics · Theory Late Subscriber Semantics
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 4221 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove one queue per subscriber responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
